title: Online Essentials
description: Internet, web browsing, email, online collaboration, and cybersecurity

questions.question: What does 'WWW' stand for?
questions.explanation: WWW (World Wide Web) is the system of interconnected documents accessed via the Internet.

questions.question: You should use the same password for all your email accounts.
questions.explanation: Each email account should have a unique, strong password to prevent unauthorized access.

questions.question: What is the primary function of a web browser?
options: To send and receive emails only
To display web pages and navigate the Internet
questions.explanation: Web browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge) are used to access and display web pages.

questions.question: It is safe to enter personal information on websites that don't have HTTPS (secure connection).
questions.explanation: HTTPS ensures encrypted communication. Avoid entering sensitive data on non-HTTPS sites.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of a search engine?
options: To index and retrieve web pages based on search terms
questions.explanation: Search engines like Google, Bing, and DuckDuckGo help find relevant web pages based on keywords.

questions.question: Which tool is best for real-time online collaboration?
questions.explanation: Cloud platforms like Google Drive, OneDrive, and Dropbox allow multiple users to work on documents simultaneously.

questions.question: Phishing emails are designed to trick users into revealing sensitive information.
questions.explanation: Phishing is a social engineering attack that uses deceptive emails to steal credentials and personal data.

questions.question: What should you always check before downloading files from the Internet?
options: The source is trustworthy and the file is scanned for malware
questions.explanation: Always verify the source and scan downloads for malware to prevent security threats.

questions.question: Bookmarking frequently visited websites helps organize your browsing and saves time.
questions.explanation: Bookmarks allow quick access to important websites without having to search each time.

questions.question: Which is the best practice for online privacy?
options: Review privacy settings and limit data sharing
Use public WiFi for sensitive transactions
questions.explanation: Managing privacy settings helps protect your personal information from unauthorized access or misuse.
